Tests the behavior of `Math.max` when passed `undefined`, expecting a result of `NaN`.

# Purpose
This code is a simple test script that uses an assertion to verify the behavior of the `Math.max` function when it receives `undefined` as an argument. The `assert` function checks if the result of `Math.max(undefined)` is `NaN` (Not-a-Number), which is the expected outcome according to JavaScript's handling of undefined values in mathematical operations. This script provides narrow functionality, focusing on a specific test case to ensure that the `Math.max` function behaves correctly in this scenario.
